Now I have a clear picture. Let me set up the infrastructure:

1. **`src/db.js`** — DB module with swappable client
2. **`src/schema.sql`** — Users table schema
3. **`src/tests/setup.js`** — PGlite-based test setup with per-test isolation
4. Install `pg` (production) and `@electric-sql/pglite` (dev)

content.text:
Everything passes. Here's what was set up:

**Files created:**

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/db.js` | Exports `db.sql` — a postgres.js tagged template. Tests swap `db.sql` to a PGlite wrapper |
| `src/schema.sql` | Users table schema (`id`, `username`, `password_hash`, `created_at`) |
| `src/tests/setup.js` | Runs `beforeEach`/`afterEach` — fresh PGlite instance per test, runs schema, seeds one `testuser` |
| `vitest.config.js` | Wires `setup.js` to run for all test files automatically |
| `src/tests/db.test.js` | Smoke test proving the setup works (seeded user exists, fresh DB per test, insert+query) |

**How it works:**
- Each test gets a brand-new in-memory Postgres (PGlite) with the schema applied and a seeded user
- `db.sql` is swapped to a wrapper that translates tagged templates into PGlite queries
- Only external calls (Jikan API) need mocking — `db.sql` calls hit real SQL against PGlite
- Controllers will use ``db.sql`SELECT * FROM users WHERE ...` `` — same syntax in prod and test
